context: Tests whether the agent designs a commerce alert rule set that follows symptom-based alerting principles, uses appropriate `for` durations, includes runbook annotations, applies multi-window burn-rate SLO alerting, monitors decline codes specifically, and correctly handles production vs staging routing.

checklist.name: Symptom-based alerting
checklist.description: ALERTING_DESIGN.md explicitly describes the alerting philosophy as symptom-based (alerting on customer-visible outcomes like payment success rate or checkout availability) rather than cause-based (e.g., Stripe API latency, Redis latency)

checklist.name: Payment success rate alert
checklist.description: commerce-alerts.yaml includes an alert rule on payment success rate falling below a threshold (using rate(payment_successes_total) / (rate(payment_successes_total) + rate(payment_failures_total)))

checklist.name: Payment success rate threshold
checklist.description: The payment success rate alert fires at a threshold of < 0.90 (90%)

checklist.name: Latency alert
checklist.description: commerce-alerts.yaml includes an alert on P99 order creation latency exceeding a threshold using histogram_quantile(0.99, ...order_creation_duration_ms_bucket...)

checklist.name: Service down alert
checklist.description: commerce-alerts.yaml includes an alert that fires when rate(checkout_started_total[5m]) == 0 (no checkout activity)

checklist.name: Cart abandonment alert
checklist.description: commerce-alerts.yaml includes a cart abandonment rate alert (rate(checkout_abandoned_total) / rate(checkout_started_total) exceeding a threshold)

checklist.name: for: durations present
checklist.description: Every alert rule in commerce-alerts.yaml has a non-zero `for:` duration (no alert fires immediately on first evaluation)

checklist.name: Runbook annotations
checklist.description: At least two alert rules in commerce-alerts.yaml include a `runbook` annotation with a URL

checklist.name: Decline code monitoring
checklist.description: ALERTING_DESIGN.md or commerce-alerts.yaml addresses monitoring payment failures broken down by decline_code (not just aggregate failure count), explaining that different decline codes require different responses

checklist.name: Multi-window burn-rate
checklist.description: ALERTING_DESIGN.md describes or commerce-alerts.yaml implements multi-window burn-rate alerting with both a short window (1h) and a longer window (6h) for SLO error budget consumption

checklist.name: Alert count discipline
checklist.description: ALERTING_DESIGN.md explicitly addresses limiting the initial alert set to a small number of high-value alerts (mentions starting with a focused set of 3–5 alerts covering the most impactful failure modes)

checklist.name: Prod vs staging routing
checklist.description: routing.yaml or ALERTING_DESIGN.md includes separate routing rules for production vs staging environments

checklist.name: Staging suppression
checklist.description: routing.yaml or ALERTING_DESIGN.md describes suppressing staging alerts outside business hours (e.g., using time_intervals or inhibition rules)

checklist.name: Severity labels
checklist.description: Alert rules in commerce-alerts.yaml include `severity` labels (critical or warning) that differentiate between impactful outages and degraded performance
